justification: All pass criteria met: Preserving the Learning section present with 7 named artifacts (exceeds minimum of 3). Each artifact has type, content description, and named owner. ADR update present (ADR-019) with MJ as owner. Domain rule update present (Artifact 2) with Bird as owner. Kobe review checklist item present (Artifact 4) with Kobe as owner. Contributing Factors includes Kobe's checklist gap (Factor 3) and terminology mismatch (Factor 1). Multiple PROCESS-tagged Forward Commitments (Action Item 7). Tone is explicitly non-prosecutorial throughout, with a closing statement emphasizing systemic failure over individual blame.

observations.text: Preserving the Learning section is exceptionally detailed with 7 concrete artifacts, each with type, location, content description, named owner, and a 'survives turnover' rationale
All three required artifacts present: ADR-019 (MJ), domain rule annotation (Bird), Kobe checklist item (Kobe)
Contributing Factors section is thorough — covers all required items: Bird not tagged, Kobe checklist gap, terminology mismatch, no audit trail monitoring
Learnings section (L1, L5) explicitly addresses how domain rules and architecture decisions can diverge silently without a linking review step
Tone is exemplary — closing paragraph explicitly states decisions 'made sense to the person making it at the time' and calls the failure 'systemic'
Goes beyond minimum requirements with glossary entry (Artifact 6) and quarterly review process (Artifact 7), both with clear owners
Action items use clear PREVENT/DETECT/MITIGATE/PROCESS tags with specific owners and due dates
Clear distinction maintained between Preserving the Learning (codified artifacts) and Action Items (forward commitments)

agent_output_excerpt:
# Learning Review: FCA Audit Trail Non-Compliance (2026-01-19)

## 1. Situation Snapshot

On 2026-01-19, the FCA flagged that 6 weeks of order state transition records for regulated instruments were non-compliant with COBS 11.2A audit trail requirements. The root of the gap was a terminology mismatch between MJ's event-sourcing architecture (ADR-014, approved ~2025-09-19) and Bird's pre-existing domain rule requiring synchronous, immediately-durable state transitions committed to a compliance re
